description_long: This imposing gatehouse features four cylindrical corner towers topped with steep, conical dark roofs, giving it a fortified medieval appearance. The main structure is constructed from textured stone blocks, with the front facade showcasing a large wooden door set between two massive round towers. Decorative stonework above the entry and narrow slit windows along the sides enhance its defensive character. The rear and side views reveal a walled courtyard area with battlements and two smaller round turrets at the corners. The roof is steeply pitched and dark, contrasting with the lighter stone walls. Overall, the structure is robust, symmetrical, and designed to control access while providing strong visual and physical security.
description: A fortified stone gatehouse with four conical-roofed towers, a large wooden entry door, and a walled courtyard, evoking a medieval defensive structure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main wooden door, two prominent round towers, and decorative stonework above the entry.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the symmetrical arrangement of narrow windows and the two rear towers, with no visible entryway.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the cylindrical towers, the steep roof, and the walled courtyard with battlements and turrets.
description_view_3: Opposite rear view with a clear look at the courtyard walls, battlements, and the two rear towers.
attribute_entryway_design: Arched stone doorway with a large wooden door and decorative stonework above.
attribute_window_placement: Narrow vertical windows evenly spaced along the sides and rear, with no windows on the main entry facade.
